# MCP Module

MCP definitions are maintained in canonical config files and synced by `xtrm install`.

## Canonical Sources

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `config/mcp_servers.json` | Core servers installed by default |
| `config/mcp_servers_optional.json` | Optional servers selected during install |

## Operational Workflow

1. Run `xtrm install` (interactive) or `xtrm install --yes`.
2. CLI syncs canonical MCP definitions for detected runtimes.
3. Optional servers are included when selected.

## Core Servers

| Server | Purpose | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| `serena` | Semantic code navigation/edit support | `uvx` |
| `context7` | Live documentation lookup | `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` |
| `github-grep` | Code search | none |
| `deepwiki` | Technical docs context | none |

## Optional Servers

| Server | Purpose | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| `unitAI` | Multi-agent orchestration | `npx` |
| `omni-search-engine` | Local SSE search backend | service on `127.0.0.1:8765` |
| `gitnexus` | Code graph intelligence | `npm i -g gitnexus` + repo indexing |

## Troubleshooting

- Missing MCP server after install: rerun `xtrm install` and confirm prompts/options.
- GitNexus unavailable in repo: `npx gitnexus analyze`.
- Context7 fails: verify `CONTEXT7_API_KEY`.
